skill.name: executing-plans
skill.description: Guides an AI assistant through saved implementation plans with critical review, batched execution, verification, and feedback checkpoints.
skill.summary: Execute saved implementation plans in reviewed, verified batches.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:24 and SKILL.md:59, but both locations are inline Markdown references to another skill. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive; the line is plain front matter text with no cryptographic code. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, filesystem access, network access, or executable commands were found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Inline Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md:24 and SKILL.md:59 contain inline Markdown code formatting for skill names, not Ruby or shell command execution. No executable code or command injection path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged characters are Markdown inline code markers around a skill name. The file contains prose instructions only, with no script runtime or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md:3 is the skill description in front matter and contains no hash, cipher, password handling, or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a natural language description. No evidence of weak cryptography or related implementation logic appears in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Implementation plans can drift when tasks are executed without review checkpoints. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through batches, verification, and feedback pauses.
actual_capabilities: Loads a saved implementation plan before work begins.
Requires critical review before executing planned tasks.
Breaks plan execution into default batches of three tasks.
Requires verification after each completed task.
Reports completed work and waits for feedback between batches.
Escalates blockers, unclear steps, and repeated verification failures.
limitations: Does not create implementation plans from scratch.
Does not execute code or tests by itself.
Depends on a separate plan document with clear steps.
Requires another skill for final branch completion.

use_cases.title: Implement a reviewed feature plan
use_cases.description: A software engineer can use the skill to keep a feature implementation aligned with a saved plan.

use_cases.title: Control agent work during long tasks
use_cases.description: A technical lead can require batch reports before the assistant continues with more changes.

use_cases.title: Verify plan progress during maintenance
use_cases.description: A maintainer can ensure each maintenance step includes verification before it is marked complete.

prompt_templates.title: Execute a simple plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a clear plan file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill to execute the plan in docs/implementation-plan.md. Review it first, then complete the first batch.

prompt_templates.title: Execute with strict verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use when tests are required after each task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill on the saved plan. Run the specified verification for each task and stop if any verification fails.

prompt_templates.title: Execute with feedback checkpoints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use when a reviewer wants control between batches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill. Complete three tasks, report what changed and what passed, then wait for my feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Execute a complex implementation plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for multi-phase implementation work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the executing-plans skill for the full implementation plan. Raise concerns before starting, follow each step exactly, and escalate blockers immediately.

output_examples.input: Execute the first three tasks from my saved plan.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the plan, records the first batch, completes each task with verification, and reports results before continuing.

output_examples.input: Continue after my feedback and stop if anything is unclear.
output_examples.output: The assistant applies the feedback, starts the next batch, and pauses when an instruction or dependency blocks progress.

output_examples.input: Finish the implementation after all plan tasks pass verification.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms all tasks are complete, then switches to the required branch completion workflow.

best_practices: Provide a plan file with small, testable tasks.
Review each batch report before asking the assistant to continue.
Include verification commands or expected checks in the plan.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill when no implementation plan exists.
Do not ask the assistant to skip verification steps.
Do not continue through blockers without clarification.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant execute a saved implementation plan in reviewed and verified batches.

faq.question: Does it write the plan for me?
faq.answer: No. It assumes a plan already exists and focuses on disciplined execution.

faq.question: How many tasks does it run at once?
faq.answer: The default batch size is three tasks before reporting and waiting for feedback.

faq.question: What happens when verification fails?
faq.answer: The assistant should stop, report the blocker, and ask for help instead of guessing.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill run external commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown workflow guide and does not contain executable command logic.
